name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Northern Arabian Sea — repositioned off eastern Oman coast, ~1,100km from Iran
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
area: Arabian Sea — continuous combat ops — CENTCOM confirms 10,000+ US targets struck
notes: Day 29: Sole operational carrier in direct theater. CENTCOM: 10,000+ US targets struck, 8,000+ combat flights, 850+ Tomahawks expended, 150+ Iranian vessels sunk (92% of large naval vessels). Iran claimed missile hit — CENTCOM denied. Sailor injured in flight ops Mar 26. CSG-3 with CVW-9 and DESRON 21 + 5 independently deployed DDGs.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23, CENTCOM 3/28, ANI News, Tribune India

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Naval Support Activity Souda Bay, Crete — pierside emergency fire repairs
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: At NSA Souda Bay, Crete for emergency pierside repairs — non-combat laundry room fire Mar 12; repair timeline unclear
notes: Day 29: Pierside at Souda Bay, Crete for repairs after March 12 non-combat fire (~200 treated for smoke inhalation). Navy says 'fully mission capable.' Expected 1+ week repairs. Bush CSG reportedly heading to Mediterranean to relieve Ford.
source: USNI News, Navy official announcement, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Naval Station Norfolk, VA — NOT yet deployed
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Pre-deployment workups — expected to relieve Ford; completing exercises
notes: Day 29: Completed COMPTUEX; certified combat-ready. Reported heading to Mediterranean to relieve Ford. No official DoD confirmation of CENTCOM orders.
source: USNI News (Mar 6), Navy Times, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Arabian Sea — in CENTCOM AOR; expected on station by end of March
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53K King Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 29: In CENTCOM AOR — Arabian Sea. Carrying 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) and approximately 20 F-35Bs from VMFA-121. Diego Garcia as of March 23, redirected from Indo-Pacific.
source: WSJ (Mar 24), USNI Fleet Tracker (Mar 23), Reuters, USNI News

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: CRITICAL: 2 of 3 LCS MCM hulls spotted in Southeast Asia (Mar 23 Maritime Executive) — MCM gap severe
vessels: LCS-30 USS Canberra (Indian Ocean — location uncertain)
LCS-16 USS Tulsa (Singapore as of Mar 18)
LCS-32 USS Santa Barbara (Singapore as of Mar 18)
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz — SEVERELY DEGRADED
notes: Day 27: Critical vulnerability persists. Iran has deployed at least a dozen mines (Maham-3 moored mines, Maham-7 bottom mines) in the Strait. 2 of 3 LCS MCM hulls spotted in Southeast Asia as of Mar 23 (Maritime Executive), thousands of miles from strait. DOT&E FY2025 report found insufficient data to determine operational effectiveness of LCS MCM mission packages. CENTCOM has destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers using A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters.
source: Maritime Executive 3/23, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23, DOT&E, CENTCOM

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Eastern Pacific — en route to Middle East
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid reinforcement — originally scheduled Indo-Pacific, rerouted and accelerated to Middle East
notes: Day 29: Departed San Diego Mar 18-20, ~3 weeks ahead of schedule. In Pacific (3rd Fleet area). ~2,500 Marines + ~4,000 total. Redirected to Middle East. ETA theater ~Apr 9.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker (Mar 23), DVIDSHUB, AP, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon

name: HMS Anson
mission_summary: UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
notes: Day 29: Royal Navy Astute-class SSN. Departed Perth March 6. Tomahawk-armed. Northern Arabian Sea (reported). No official UK confirmation of exact position.

name: 82nd Airborne Division
area: En route to CENTCOM — ~2,000-3,000 paratroopers — WSJ: up to 10,000 more considered
mission_summary: Ground combat force — HQ elements, division enablers, and 1st BCT deploying to CENTCOM; IRF can deploy within 18 hours
notes: Day 29: ~2,000 soldiers from 1st BCT ordered March 24. Immediate Response Force designed to deploy within 18 hours. Trump considering sending 10,000 additional combat troops. Total US forces in region ~50,000.
source: Stars and Stripes Mar 25, CBS News, CNN, NPR Mar 26

name: HMS Dragon
notes: Day 29: Type 45 destroyer. Departed Portsmouth March 10 for air defense mission in Eastern Mediterranean.

name: FS Charles de Gaulle
mission_summary: French carrier operations — Eastern Mediterranean. France pledged 8 frigates + 2 amphibious assault ships.
notes: Day 29: France pledged 8 frigates + 2 amphibious assault ships for coalition operations. Charles de Gaulle operating in Eastern Mediterranean.

name: VMFA-311 Marine F-35Cs
area: RAF Lakenheath, UK — staging en route to CENTCOM
aircraft: F-35C Lightning II (first 5 of 10 arrived Mar 23-24; first-ever land-based F-35C combat deployment)
mission_summary: Marine F-35C squadron staging through UK en route to CENTCOM theater
notes: Day 27: The War Zone confirmed Mar 24 that first 5 of 10 planned F-35Cs from VMFA-311 'Tomcats' arrived RAF Lakenheath Mar 23-24, having departed MCAS Miramar Mar 10. First-ever land-based combat deployment of the F-35C. Wikipedia states all 10 arrived; second batch of 5 UNVERIFIED in primary reporting. Also notable: AC-130J Ghostrider gunship arrived Lakenheath from Iceland Mar 24.
source: The War Zone Mar 24, USNI News, Pentagon
